name: Advanced Design System Patterns
description: Advanced concepts for building, scaling, and maintaining enterprise design systems
note: Builds on ux://design-systems/tokens. This covers advanced topics like theming, multi-brand, versioning, and governance.

semantic_tokens.description: Tokens that reference other tokens and carry meaning
benefits: Change theme by swapping semantic tokens
Single source of truth for colors/spacing
Easier maintenance (change blue-500 once, updates everywhere)

approach_1_separate_tokens.description: Define separate tokens for light and dark

multi_brand.description: Support multiple brands from one design system

structure.brand_specific: Colors, logos, border radius, fonts (per brand)

implementation.build_time: Generate separate CSS bundles per brand (webpack, Sass)
implementation.runtime: Swap theme object in JavaScript (Styled Components, Emotion)

multi_brand.example: Carbon Design System (IBM), multiple product brands

system_preference.override: Allow user to override system preference

responsive_tokens.description: Tokens that change based on viewport

fluid_typography.description: Font size scales smoothly between min and max viewport

responsive_spacing.recommended: Approach 1 for discrete jumps, Approach 2 for smooth scaling

compound_components.description: Components that work together with shared context

example.tabs: Tabs container shares state with Tab and TabPanel children
example.dropdown: Dropdown provides context to Trigger and Menu
example.accordion: Accordion manages state for multiple AccordionItem children

implementation.react: Use Context API to share state between parent and children

polymorphic_components.description: Components that can render as different HTML elements
polymorphic_components.use_case: Button component that can render as <button>, <a>, or <Link>

controlled_uncontrolled.description: Support both controlled and uncontrolled modes

controlled.use_when: Need to validate, transform, or sync with other state

uncontrolled.use_when: Simple forms, don't need to control state

hybrid.pattern: If 'value' prop provided, use it (controlled). Else, use internal state (uncontrolled).

process.1_announce: Announce deprecation in release notes, changelog
process.2_warn: Add console warnings in deprecated components/props
process.4_grace_period: At least 2 minor versions before removal (e.g., deprecated in 2.1, removed in 3.0)

codemods.use_when: Large breaking changes affecting many consumers

changelog.example:
## [2.1.0] - 2024-01-15
### Added
- New `Toast` component for notifications
- `size` prop to `Button` component

### Fixed
- `Modal` close button now keyboard accessible

### Deprecated
- `OldButton` (use `Button` instead)

migration_guides.description: Step-by-step guides for major version upgrades
migration_guides.example: Material-UI v4 to v5 migration guide (comprehensive)

essential_sections.overview: What the component does, when to use
essential_sections.accessibility: ARIA attributes, keyboard support, screen reader behavior

contribution_guide.description: How to contribute to the design system

style_dictionary.description: Transform design tokens to multiple platforms
style_dictionary.output: CSS, Sass, JS, iOS, Android, etc.
style_dictionary.use: Single source of truth for all platforms

figma_tokens.description: Sync tokens between Figma and code
figma_tokens.workflow: Design in Figma → Export tokens → Generate code

visual_regression.workflow: Take screenshots → Compare with baseline → Approve/reject

accessibility_testing.ci: Run axe in CI, fail on violations

linting.custom_rules: Enforce design system usage (no hardcoded colors, use tokens)

build_tools.tree_shaking: Ensure components can be tree-shaken (ESM + sideEffects: false)

core_team.description: Full-time design system engineers and designers

rfc_process.description: Request for Comments - propose new components/changes

process.1_proposal: Write RFC document (problem, solution, alternatives, API)
process.2_review: Core team + working group review, provide feedback

track: Design system version (how many on latest)

multi_framework.problem: Teams use React, Vue, Angular, Svelte, etc.

multiple_implementations.example: Carbon Design System (React, Vue, Angular, Svelte, Web Components)

cons: No visual consistency out of box

legacy_migration.problem: Migrating old products to design system

incremental.description: Migrate page by page, component by component

coexistence.description: Run old and new design system side-by-side
coexistence.example: Namespace old styles with .legacy, new with .ds

component_composition.example: Card component built from Box, Text, Button primitives

utility_first.use_with_ds: Use for layout, DS for components

examples.visually_hidden: Hide content visually, keep for screen readers

material_ui.approach: Themeable, extensive component set, multiple packages

carbon_ibm.scale: Multiple frameworks (React, Vue, Angular, Svelte, Web Components)
carbon_ibm.approach: Design tokens, extensive guidelines, governance model

lessons: Opinionated DS can work (less choice = consistency)

atlassian_design_system.approach: Extensive token system, dark mode, rigorous accessibility

best_practices: Use semantic tokens (3-tier: core → semantic → component)
Write migration guides for major versions
Use Storybook or similar for component explorer
Use compound components for related UI
Consider multi-framework if needed (Web Components or multiple implementations)
Incremental migration strategy for legacy apps
Publish to npm with proper versioning
anti_patterns: Building components no one needs (ivory tower)
No migration guides (consumers stuck on old versions)
Releasing breaking changes in minor versions
Single framework lock-in (when org uses multiple)
No changelog (consumers don't know what changed)
Skipping visual regression tests (unintended changes ship)
